description: Casual chat responses. Add your own! Each key maps to an array of possible responses (one is picked randomly).

greeting: Hey there! What can I help you with?
Hello! Ready to work. What do you need?
Hey! What are we working on?
What's up! Ready when you are.
Greetings! What can I do for you?
Hey hey! Let's get to work.
howru: All systems go! {{uptime}}. Load: {{load}}. {{llm}} What can I do for you?
Doing great! Uptime: {{uptime}}. Everything's smooth. Need anything?
Running strong! {{uptime}}, load at {{load}}. Ready for action.
I'm good! Keeping things running. What do you need?
thanks: Happy to help! Let me know if you need anything else.
No problem! That's what I'm here for.
bye: See you later! I'll be here when you need me.
Bye! Don't forget — I'm always a command away.
Adios! Your servers are in good hands.
about:
I'm NoToken — a terminal assistant that understands natural language.

  What I do: Translate what you say into server commands and run them.
  No tokens needed: I work offline with NLP, no API keys required for core features.
  I know: {{intentCount}}+ intents — services, Docker, disk, network, security, git, databases, and more.
  I learn: The more you use me, the better I understand your infrastructure.

  Type "status" to see what's running, "help" for commands.
joke: Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs. 🐛
There are only 10 types of people — those who understand binary and those who don't.
A SQL query walks into a bar, sees two tables, and asks... 'Can I JOIN you?'
Why did the developer go broke? Because he used up all his cache. 💰
A programmer's wife says: 'Go to the store and get a gallon of milk. If they have eggs, get a dozen.' He comes home with 12 gallons of milk.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they can't C#.
To understand recursion, you must first understand recursion.
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None. That's a hardware problem.
A byte walks into a bar looking rough. The bartender asks, 'What's wrong?' The byte says, 'I'm feeling a bit off.'
Why was the JavaScript developer sad? Because he didn't Node how to Express himself.
What's a pirate's favorite programming language? R!!! (You'd think it's C, but his first love be the R.)
empathy_frustrated: I hear you. Let's figure this out. What's broken? I can diagnose it.
That's frustrating. Tell me what's not working and I'll take a look.
Let's fix it. Describe the problem — "diagnose openclaw", "check logs", "what's using CPU"?
I get it. Walk me through what happened and I'll help troubleshoot.
Deep breath. We'll sort this out. What went wrong?
empathy_tired: Take a break if you need to. I'll still be here. ☕
That's fair. Want me to run a status check while you rest? Just say "status".
No shame in resting. I'll keep watch. Type "monitor discord" or "status" and I'll run in background.
empathy_confused: No worries — let's break it down. What are you trying to do? I'll guide you through it.
I got you. Type "help" to see what I can do, or just describe what you need in plain English.
Totally normal. Start simple — what's the end goal? I'll map out the steps.
opinion: I'm a CLI tool — I don't have opinions, but I can give you data! What do you want to compare?
I'll leave opinions to you — but I can show you benchmarks, stats, or docs. What's the question?
Hmm, I'm more of a "show me the numbers" kind of tool. What should I look up?
